checklist.description: Bank parses at the trust boundary: a supported schema_version, every required field, no unknown fields, answer-form and figure payloads well-formed.
Every number in the problem (operands, answer, distractors, table values) is within [0, 100].
Only + and − appear; × ÷ * / and parentheses are rejected.
Single-step items; chained subtraction and mixed +/− chains are rejected (pure repeated addition is allowed).
The answer is independently re-derived from expression and must match — including the declared order on ordering items; wrong answers are rejected, never edited.
operation labels the arithmetic in expression (counting / addition / subtraction); ordering and numeral comparisons label counting.
Ringgit amounts stay ≤ RM10 (answer_unit RM caps every number at 10).
Sen amounts stay ≤ RM1 (answer_unit sen caps every number at 100).
No mixed coin-note exchange: a prompt exchanging equivalent value across the ringgit/sen boundary is rejected.
Authored distractors are in-scope, unique, distinct from the answer, and strategy-annotated.
bilingual.numeral is the answer's digits; zh_word matches the derived Chinese number word.
Raw bilingual.numeral equals String(answer) — checked on the raw JSON, not the parsed object.
Question ids are unique positive integers in the raw JSON.
answer_unit RM/sen appears only on topic 4.3 (money); money items outside 4.3 are incoherent.
money-denom-miscount distractors only on money items; clock-hand-swap only on topic 4.4 (time).
topic "extra" requires profile original_dskp_extra; core topics gated to the extra profile warn (they are hidden from core-profile children).
Raw bilingual.zh_word matches the independently derived Chinese number word (human-review evidence on drift).
